Create aliases in `~/.bashrc` for quick access

walkthroughs.title: GGUF v3 Quantization (Q4_0, Q4_K, Q5_K, Q8_0)
walkthroughs.description: Deep dive into GGUF v3 quantization formats: when to use Q4_0 vs Q4_K vs Q5_K vs Q8_0, SIMD decode pipelines, and attention engine bottlenecks.
steps: Understand quantization: reducing weight precision (fp16 → int4/int8) with minimal quality loss
Q4_0: Fastest, smallest, legacy format — good for CPU-only inference
Q4_K: K-quant — grouped quantization, better perplexity than Q4_0 at similar size
Q5_K: Higher precision, ~25% larger than Q4_K — sweet spot for quality/speed
Q8_0: Near-fp16 quality, ~2x size of Q4_K — use when accuracy critical
Profile attention: check `ggml_cycles` output for kernel bottlenecks

about.title: About Living ASCII Art
about.description: A terminal-aesthetic tech dashboard that fetches live headlines and renders them as animated rainbow-wave ASCII art. Built with vanilla HTML/CSS/JS — no frameworks, no build step.
features: Live tech headlines → ASCII art (rainbow wave animation, no fade)
GitHub project tracking with next-step issues
Responsive — works on mobile Termux, desktop, tablet
tech_stack: GitHub Actions for automated content refresh

contact.note: Open to collaboration on AI tooling, terminal UIs, quantization research, and sovereign compute.
